name: Bugis
notes: Buginese (Basa Ugi) — South Sulawesi (Austronesian) language of Indonesia. Traditionally written in the Lontara script, a Brahmic abugida (each consonant letter carries an inherent /a/ modified by vowel diacritics; final consonants and geminates are systematically not written), and today also in the Latin script. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED for either script: the Lontara defective encoding of codas and gemination makes a reliable grapheme→IPA table a research task. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Lontara script (Unicode block Buginese) or in the Latin orthography.
